title: Dual frame eyeglasses
abstract: The disclosure herein describes an apparatus for dual-frame eyeglasses that include a foundation eyeglass frame that may be worn on the user's head, and a pivot eyeglass frame that is rigidly coupled to the foundation eyeglass frame via a pair of pivot mechanisms. The pivot eyeglass frame may be substantially similar in form and profile to the foundation eyeglass frame. The pivot eyeglass frame may be configured to incrementally rotate about the pivot mechanism from a first position that substantially overlaps a user's line of sight through to a second position that is angularly offset away from the user's line of sight. Additionally, the foundation eyeglass frame may include a nose bridge that separates into two elements at a coupling point on the nose bridge to facilitate each half of the foundation eyeglass frame folding into a separate, compact form.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED: 1. A pair of dual-frame eyeglasses, comprising: a pivot eyeglass frame that includes a pivot-lens support frame for holding a left focus lens unit and a right focus lens unit, the pivot eyeglass frame further comprising: a left pivot arm that is rigidly fixed to a left outer portion of the pivot-lens support frame via a first left hinge, the left pivot arm being further rigidly fixed to a left pivot mechanism; and a right pivot arm that is rigidly fixed to a right outer portion of the pivot-lens support frame via a first right hinge, the right pivot arm being further rigidly fixed to a right pivot mechanism; a foundation eyeglass frame that includes a foundation-lens support frame that is substantially similar to a portion of the pivot-lens support frame, the foundation eyeglass frame further comprising: a left temple arm that is rigidly fixed to a left outer portion of the foundation eyeglass frame via a second left hinge, the left temple arm being further rigidly fixed to the left pivot mechanism; and a right temple arm that is rigidly fixed to a right outer portion of the foundation eyeglass frame via a second right hinge, the right temple arm being further rigidly fixed to the right pivot mechanism; the left pivot mechanism for coupling the left pivot arm and the left temple arm; and the right pivot mechanism for coupling the right pivot arm and the right temple arm. 2. The pair of dual-frame eyeglasses of claim 1, wherein the left pivot mechanism is configured to enable incremental angular offsets of the left pivot arm relative to the left temple arm, and further configured to restrict translational movement of the left pivot arm relative to the left temple arm, and 

 the right pivot mechanism being configured to enable incremental angular offsets of the right pivot arm relative to the left temple arm, and further configured to restrict translational movement of the right pivot arm relative to the right temple arm. 3. The pair of dual-frame eyeglasses of claim 1, wherein the left pivot mechanism comprises of a first base plate and a first pivot plate, the first base plate being rigidly fixed to a particular location on the left temple arm of the foundation eyeglass frame, the first base plate further including a plurality of first magnets having a first polarity, wherein, the first pivot plate is rigidly fixed to a free end of the left pivot arm of the pivot eyeglass frame, the first pivot plate including a plurality of second magnets that have a second polarity that is opposite of the first polarity, and further comprising: a fastening system that couples the first base plate to the first pivot plate through a rotational centroid of the first pivot plate, and wherein the right pivot mechanism is substantially a mirror image of the left pivot mechanism. 4. The pair of dual-frame eyeglasses of claim 3, wherein the plurality of first magnets of the foundation eyeglass frame corresponds to a number of first magnets that is double a number of second magnets that is associated with the plurality of second magnets of the pivot eyeglass frame. 5. The pair of dual-frame eyeglasses of claim 3, wherein the particular location that the first base plate is rigidly fixed to the left temple arm is based at least in part on enabling the pivot-lens support frame of the pivot eyeglass frame to substantially overlap the foundation-lens support frame of the foundation eyeglass frame in response to a downward rotation of the pivot eyeglass frame relative to the foundation eyeglass frame. 6. The pair of dual -frame eyeglasses of claim 1, wherein the foundation eyeglass frame further comprises a nose bridge, the nose bridge comprising a left longitudinal member and a right longitudinal member, wherein: the left longitudinal member comprises a left first end and a left second end, the left first end being rigidly fixed to a left inner portion of the foundation-lens support frame, and the left second end having a rigidly fixed first magnet, the right longitudinal member comprises a right first end and a right second end, the right first end being rigidly fixed to a right inner portion of the foundation-lens support frame, and the right second end having a rigidly fixed second magnet, and the first magnet having a first polarity that is opposite to a second polarity of the second magnet. 7. The pair of dual-frame eyeglasses of claim 1, further comprising: a left arm catch that is rigidly fixed to the left pivot arm of the pivot eyeglass frame, the left arm catch protruding inward towards the left temple arm of the foundation eyeglass frame such that the left arm catch abuts the left temple arm in response to a downward rotation of the pivot eyeglass frame relative to the foundation eyeglass frame; and a right arm catch that is rigidly fixed to the right pivot arm of the pivot eyeglass frame, the right arm catch protruding inward towards the right temple arm of the foundation eyeglass frame such that the right arm catch abuts the right temple arm in response to the downward rotation of the pivot eyeglass frame relative to the foundation eyeglass frame. 8. An eyeglass frame assembly, comprising: a pivot eyeglass frame that includes a pivot-lens support frame for holding a left focus lens unit and a right focus lens unit, the pivot eyeglass frame further comprising a left pivot arm and a right pivot arm, wherein: 

 the left pivot arm is rigidly fixed to a left outer portion of the pivot-lens support frame via a first left hinge, the left pivot arm including a first left pivot element and a first left alignment element, the right pivot arm is rigidly fixed to a right outer portion of the pivot-lens support frame via a first right hinge, the right pivot arm including a first right pivot element and a first right alignment element; and a foundation eyeglass frame that includes a foundation-lens support frame that is substantially similar to a portion of the pivot-lens support frame, the foundation eyeglass frame further comprising a left temple arm and a right temple arm, wherein: the left temple arm is rigidly fixed to a left outer portion of the foundation-lens support frame via a second left hinge, the left temple arm including a second left pivot element and second left alignment elements, the right temple arm is rigidly fixed to a right outer portion of the foundation-lens support frame via a second right hinge, the right temple arm including a second right pivot element and second right alignment elements, and wherein the pivot eyeglass frame is selectively coupled to the foundation eyeglass frame via an interaction between the first left pivot element and the second left pivot element, the first right pivot element and the second right pivot element, the first left alignment element and one of the second left alignment elements, and the first right alignment element and one of the second right alignment elements. 9. The eyeglass frame assembly of claim 8, wherein the first left pivot element, the first right pivot element, the first left alignment element, and the first right alignment element of the pivot eyeglass frame comprise of first magnets that have a first polarity, wherein, the second left pivot element, the second right pivot element, the second left alignment elements, and the second right alignment elements of the foundation eyeglass frame comprise of second magnets that have a second polarity, and 

 wherein, the first polarity of the first magnets is opposite to the second polarity of the second magnets. 10. The eyeglass frame assembly of claim 9, wherein the first magnets nest within first recess seats of the left pivot arm and the right pivot arm, the first recess seats being sized to be substantially similar to a perimeter geometry and depth of the first magnets, and wherein the second magnets nest within second recess seats of the left temple arm and the right temple arm, the second recess seats being sized to be substantially similar to a perimeter geometry and depth of the second magnets. 11. The eyeglass frame assembly of claim 8, wherein the left temple arm includes a left fingerlike protrusion for nesting a second left alignment element of the second left alignment elements, the left fingerlike protrusion being sized to orient the second left alignment element in a position that enables the second left alignment element to couple with the first left alignment element of the pivot eyeglass frame, a coupling of the second left alignment element and the first left alignment element to occur when the first left pivot element of the pivot eyeglass frame couples with the second left pivot element of the foundation eyeglass frame, and wherein, the right temple arm includes a right fingerlike protrusion that is substantially a mirror image of the left fingerlike protrusion of the left temple arm. 12. The eyeglass frame assembly of claim 8, wherein the first left pivot element of the pivot eyeglass frame is located at a free end of the left pivot arm, the free end being opposite to first left hinge that is rigidly fixed to the pivot-lens support frame, and wherein, the first right pivot element is located at a free end of the right pivot arm, the free end being opposite to the first right hinge that is rigidly fixed to the pivot-lens support frame. 13. The eyeglass frame assembly of claim 8, wherein a second left alignment element of the second left alignment elements nests within a recess seat of the left temple arm such that a coupling of the second left alignment element to the first left alignment element of the pivot eyeglass frame enables the pivot-lens support frame of the pivot eyeglass frame to substantially overlap the foundation-lens support frame of the foundation eyeglass frame in response to a downward rotation of the pivot eyeglass frame relative to the foundation eyeglass frame, and wherein a second right alignment element of the second right alignment elements nests within a substantially similar recess seat of the right temple arm. 14. A dual-frame eyeglass apparatus, comprising a pivot eyeglass frame and a foundation eyeglass frame, wherein: a pivot eyeglass frame that includes a pivot-lens support frame for holding a left focus lens unit and a right focus lens unit, the pivot eyeglass frame further comprising a left pivot arm and a right pivot arm, wherein: the left pivot arm is rigidly fixed to a left outer portion of the pivot-lens support frame via a first left hinge, the left pivot arm including a first left pivot magnet and a first left alignment magnet, the first left pivot magnet and the first left alignment magnet having a first polarity, and the right pivot arm is rigidly fixed to a right outer portion of the pivot-lens support frame via a first right hinge, the right pivot arm including a first right pivot magnet and a first right alignment magnet, the first right pivot magnet and the first right alignment magnet having the first polarity; a foundation eyeglass frame that includes a foundation-lens support frame that is substantially similar to a portion of the pivot-lens support frame, the foundation eyeglass frame further comprising a left temple arm and a right temple arm, wherein: the left temple arm is rigidly fixed to a left outer portion of the foundation-lens support frame via a second left hinge, the left temple arm having a left fingerlike protrusion for nesting second left alignment magnets, the left temple arm further including 

 a second left pivot magnet, the second left pivot magnet and the second left alignment magnets having a second polarity that is opposite the first polarity, the right temple arm is rigidly fixed to a right outer portion of the foundation-lens support frame via a second right hinge, the right temple arm having a right fingerlike protrusion for nesting second right alignment magnets, the right temple arm further including a second right pivot magnet, the second right pivot magnet and the second right alignment magnets having a second polarity that is opposite the first polarity, and wherein, the pivot eyeglass frame is selectively coupled to the foundation eyeglass frame via an interaction between the first left pivot element and the second left pivot magnet, the first right pivot magnet and the second right pivot magnet, the first left alignment magnet and one of the second left alignment magnets, and the first right alignment magnet and one of the second right alignment magnets. 15. The dual-frame eyeglass apparatus of claim 14, wherein the left temple arm includes a straight portion and a bent portion, the straight portion extending rearward towards a free end of the left temple arm and a bent portion that extends forward from the free end, the bent portion formed to partially overlap a user's ear, wherein, the second left pivot magnet is located on the straight portion of the left temple arm, and wherein, the second right pivot magnet is located on a substantially similar straight portion of the right temple arm as the second left pivot magnet on the left temple arm.
